---
title: "Getting started with claude code"
author: "Coady Wing"
date: "2026-03-06"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: false
    mainfont: "Lato, sans-serif"
    monofont: "SF Mono, Fira Code, Consolas, monospace"
    css: hackathon-style.css
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

# Introduction

How many weird things can you do with claude code in a short
time? Which things will work well and which will fail? How do
you get claude to help you?

---

## Task 1: Twenty Years of Poverty in the Midwest

1. Make a new project directory on your computer. Add
   sub-directories for standard things: `raw_data`,
   `intermediate_data`, `code`, `output`, `manuscript`,
   `notes`.

2. Open the terminal and change directory to your project.
   Launch claude.

3. Tell claude that you are starting a new project that will
   use data from the USDA Economic Research Service to compare
   county level poverty rates in the midwestern United States
   over the past 20 years.

   - Ask claude to write a `CLAUDE.md` file that explains this
     basic context and the directory structure.
   - Also tell it that you will eventually put this project
     into a github repository, so it should write a `README.md`
     file that will help users run the code and reproduce
     results.

4. Write a prompt asking claude to:

   - download the relevant data from the USDA
   - write R code to make maps showing the poverty rate in each
     county today and 20 years ago

   Tell it to enter plan mode and figure out how to proceed.
   Add any instructions you may have about coding preferences
   and documentation. When you are ready, run the plan.

5. Look at the results and iterate as needed. Write up a short report in latex and compile the paper.

## Task 2: Reimplement Stata Code in R

The `ai_workflow_seminar` repository has some example materials. 

- Have a look at `.claude/rules/r-code-conventions.md` for ideas about how to get claude to write code the way you want.

- Take the Stata `.do` files in `example_materials` and ask Claude to reimplement them in R. If you have your own Stata code, use that instead.

## Task 3: Domain Expert Review

Have a look at `.claude/agents/domain-reviewer.md`. It is designed to review your manuscript pretending to be a fussy empirical economist. 

Have Claude conduct a domain-expert review of a draft paper. (It does have an easier time with plaintext or docx files; it does not love pdf files as much.)

## Task 4: Build a Custom Skill, Agent, or Rule

Look at existing skills, agents, and rules in `.claude/` for
ideas. Ask claude to help you.

## Task 5: Academic Website on GitHub

Ask Claude to build you a personal academic website and deploy it to GitHub Pages. The site should include:

- Your name and photo placeholder
- Research interests
- CV or resume section
- Links (email, Google Scholar, GitHub, etc.)
